summary: ByteDance Seed launched Seed 2.0 alongside its Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM and the Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, signaling a serious push into real-time voice interaction and broader foundation-model deployment. Zhipu AI unveiled GLM-5 as its new flagship agent-engineering model, claiming open-source state-of-the-art coding and agent performance with results comparable to Claude Opus 4.5 on SWE-bench Verified and Terminal Bench 2.0. On the research side, "Judge Circuits" highlighted a core reliability issue in LLM-as-a-judge evaluation by showing score shifts caused purely by output-format changes, a meaningful warning for teams relying on automated benchmarking.

top_topics.HEADLINE: Seed 2.0 Launch Puts ByteDance Seed Into Real-Time Multimodal Competition
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: ByteDance Seed announced three linked artifacts at once: the core Seed 2.0 model, the Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, and the Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview release on Arena for early evaluation.

The Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM was positioned around attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural interaction, indicating a focus on low-latency, interruption-tolerant voice experiences rather than text-only chat.

Releasing Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena suggests ByteDance wants external comparison and developer visibility early, a common tactic when vendors are trying to establish benchmark credibility and ecosystem pull.

items.title: GLM-5 announced as Zhipu AI's new flagship foundation model for agent engineering
items.summary: Zhipu AI announced GLM-5 as a flagship base model aimed at agent engineering, highlighting open-source SOTA performance in coding and agent capabilities. The page also claims strong results on SWE-bench Verified and Terminal Bench 2.0, comparable to Claude Opus 4.5.

top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: GLM-5 strengthens the China open-model ecosystem and gives enterprises another serious candidate for coding and agent workflows outside the U.S. frontier-model stack.
top_topics.HEADLINE: GLM-5 Becomes Zhipu AI’s Flagship Model for Agent Engineering
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: Zhipu AI presented GLM-5 as its new flagship foundation model specifically optimized for agent engineering, not just general chatbot use.
The announcement claims open-source state-of-the-art performance in coding and agent tasks, with benchmark references including SWE-bench Verified and Terminal Bench 2.0.

Zhipu explicitly framed GLM-5 as comparable to Claude Opus 4.5 on those evaluations, signaling an attempt to compete directly with premium closed-model incumbents in software and autonomous task execution.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: This item says Android is entering a Gemini Intelligence era, indicating a new phase of Gemini integration across the Android platform. It points to a product update centered on embedding Google's AI features more deeply into Android experiences.
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: Google’s Gemini integration into Android signals that model advantage is increasingly being converted into distribution advantage through operating systems and consumer hardware.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Gemini Intelligence Rollout Extends Google’s AI Stack Across Android Devices
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: Coverage from The Rundown AI and Superhuman AI indicates Google is pushing Gemini-branded intelligence deeper into Android, broadening exposure beyond standalone chatbot products.
One cited angle described a 'Gemini-powered laptop,' implying Google’s AI positioning now spans both mobile and adjacent device categories rather than remaining confined to phones.

For enterprise buyers and investors, the key implication is that Google can bundle Gemini into default user experiences at platform scale, which may lower customer-acquisition costs relative to standalone AI apps.

top_topics.title: Judge Circuits
top_topics.summary: LLM-as-a-judge has become the dominant paradigm for grading model outputs at scale, yet the same model assigns systematically different scores when its output format changes (e.g., a 1-5 rating vs. a True/False label). Existing diagnoses of these format-induced inconsistencies stop at the input-output level. Using Position-aware Edge Attribution Patching (PEAP), we causally investigate the internal mechanism in Gemma-3, Qwen2.5, and Llama-3. We find that judgments across structured understanding and open-ended preference tasks share a sparse, generalized Latent Evaluator sub-graph in the mi...

items.title: Judge Circuits
items.summary: LLM-as-a-judge has become the dominant paradigm for grading model outputs at scale, yet the same model assigns systematically different scores when its output format changes (e.g., a 1-5 rating vs. a True/False label). Existing diagnoses of these format-induced inconsistencies stop at the input-output level. Using Position-aware Edge Attribution Patching (PEAP), we causally investigate the internal mechanism in Gemma-3, Qwen2.5, and Llama-3. We find that judgments across structured understanding and open-ended preference tasks share a sparse, generalized Latent Evaluator sub-graph in the mi...

metadata.comment: Identifies sparse latent evaluator circuits that separate abstract judgment computation from format-specific output branches.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: LLM-as-a-judge has become the dominant paradigm for grading model outputs at scale, yet the same model assigns systematically different scores when its output format changes (e.g., a 1-5 rating vs. a True/False label). Existing diagnoses of these format-induced inconsistencies stop at the input-output level. Using Position-aware Edge Attribution Patching (PEAP), we causally investigate the internal mechanism in Gemma-3, Qwen2.5, and Llama-3. We find that judgments across structured understanding and open-ended preference tasks share a sparse, generalized Latent Evaluator sub-graph in the mi...
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: If automated evaluators change their scores based on output format rather than substance, product, research, and procurement decisions built on those evaluations can be materially distorted.
top_topics.HEADLINE: Judge Circuits Paper Exposes Format Instability in LLM-as-a-Judge Systems
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: "Judge Circuits" examines the increasingly important LLM-as-a-judge setup and finds that the same model can score identical content differently when the response schema changes, such as 1-5 ratings versus binary labels.
The paper pushes beyond surface-level observations of benchmark inconsistency by analyzing the mechanism behind these formatting effects, making it more actionable for model-evaluation teams.

The result matters for labs and enterprises using judge models in reinforcement learning, internal eval suites, and leaderboard reporting, where apparent gains may reflect prompt or format artifacts rather than genuine capability improvements.

topics.title: Syntax Without Semantics: Teaching Large Language Models to Code in an Unseen Language
topics.summary: Large language models (LLMs) achieve high pass rates on code generation benchmarks, yet whether they can transfer this ability to languages absent from pretraining remains poorly understood. We introduce PyLang, a minimal imperative language absent from all pretraining corpora, and evaluate frontier models zero-shot and fine-tuned Qwen3 (4B, 8B, 32B) on 352 problems. We find that fine-tuning quickly teaches syntax but fails to transfer semantic competence: Python outperforms PyLang by up to 19% across all configurations, and no intervention (multi-task learning, preference tuning, code infi...

items.title: Syntax Without Semantics: Teaching Large Language Models to Code in an Unseen Language
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) achieve high pass rates on code generation benchmarks, yet whether they can transfer this ability to languages absent from pretraining remains poorly understood. We introduce PyLang, a minimal imperative language absent from all pretraining corpora, and evaluate frontier models zero-shot and fine-tuned Qwen3 (4B, 8B, 32B) on 352 problems. We find that fine-tuning quickly teaches syntax but fails to transfer semantic competence: Python outperforms PyLang by up to 19% across all configurations, and no intervention (multi-task learning, preference tuning, code infi...

metadata.comment: The paper identifies an implementation fidelity gap: models retain language-agnostic algorithmic representations but fail to realize them in an unseen programming language.

topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Large language models (LLMs) achieve high pass rates on code generation benchmarks, yet whether they can transfer this ability to languages absent from pretraining remains poorly understood. We introduce PyLang, a minimal imperative language absent from all pretraining corpora, and evaluate frontier models zero-shot and fine-tuned Qwen3 (4B, 8B, 32B) on 352 problems. We find that fine-tuning quickly teaches syntax but fails to transfer semantic competence: Python outperforms PyLang by up to 19% across all configurations, and no intervention (multi-task learning, preference tuning, code infi...

topics.title: $\phi$-Balancing for Mixture-of-Experts Training
topics.summary: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models rely on balanced expert utilization to fully realize their scalability. However, existing load-balancing methods are largely heuristic and operate on noisy mini-batch assignment statistics, introducing bias relative to population-level objectives. We propose $\phi$-balancing, a principled framework that directly targets population-level expert balance by minimizing a strictly convex, symmetric, and differentiable potential of the expected routing distribution. Using convex duality, we derive an equivalent min-max formulation and obtain a simple online algorit...

items.title: $\phi$-Balancing for Mixture-of-Experts Training

Using convex duality, we derive an equivalent min-max formulation and obtain a simple online algorit...

metadata.comment: Replaces heuristic MoE load balancing with a population-level convex potential objective and mirror-descent routing adjustment.

topics.title: A Few GPUs, A Whole Lotta Scale: Faithful LLM Training Emulation with PrismLLM
topics.summary: Large language model (LLM) training today runs on clusters spanning thousands of GPUs. While this scale enables rapid model advances, developing, debugging, and performance-tuning the training framework inevitably becomes complex and costly. This is because engineers often need to reproduce production behaviors to diagnose failures or evaluate optimizations, thereby demanding frequent and even exclusive access to production-scale clusters -- which becomes increasingly hard given that the majority of GPUs are already committed to production workloads. Simulation relies on complex performance...

topics.title: Agentic Discovery of Neural Architectures: AIRA-Compose and AIRA-Design
topics.summary: Toward recursive self-improvement, we investigate LLM agents autonomously designing foundation models beyond standard Transformers. We introduce a dual-framework approach: AIRA-Compose for high-level architecture search, and AIRA-Design for low-level mechanistic implementation. AIRA-Compose uses 11 agents to explore fundamental computational primitives under a 24-hour budget. Agents evaluate million-parameter candidates, extrapolating top designs to 350M, 1B, and 3B scales. This yields 14 architectures across two families: AIRAformers (Transformer-based) and AIRAhybrids (Transformer-Mamba)....

items.title: Agentic Discovery of Neural Architectures: AIRA-Compose and AIRA-Design
items.summary: Toward recursive self-improvement, we investigate LLM agents autonomously designing foundation models beyond standard Transformers. We introduce a dual-framework approach: AIRA-Compose for high-level architecture search, and AIRA-Design for low-level mechanistic implementation. AIRA-Compose uses 11 agents to explore fundamental computational primitives under a 24-hour budget. Agents evaluate million-parameter candidates, extrapolating top designs to 350M, 1B, and 3B scales. This yields 14 architectures across two families: AIRAformers (Transformer-based) and AIRAhybrids (Transformer-Mamba)....

metadata.comment: Agent-driven discovery of new Transformer and Transformer-Mamba architectures, including attention mechanisms and scaling-efficient designs.

topics.title: BAPR: Bayesian amnesic piecewise-robust reinforcement learning for non-stationary continuous control
topics.summary: Real-world control systems frequently operate under \emph{piecewise stationary} conditions, where dynamics remain stable for extended periods before undergoing abrupt regime changes. Standard robust RL methods face a fundamental dilemma: a globally conservative policy wastes performance during stable periods, while a locally adaptive policy risks catastrophic failure when the regime changes undetected. We propose \textbf{BAPR} (Bayesian Amnesic Piecewise-Robust SAC), which unifies Bayesian Online Change Detection (BOCD) with robust ensemble RL. The BAPR operator -- a convex combination of m...

topics.title: GDS weighs in on the NHS's decision to retreat from Open Source
topics.summary:
GDS weighs in on the NHS's decision to retreat from Open Source

Terence Eden continues his coverage of the NHS' poorly considered decision to close down access to their open source repositories in response to vulnerabilities reported to them as part of Project Glasswing.

Now the Government Digital Service have joined the conversation with AI, open code and vulnerability risk in the public sector

paper_spotlight.label: New Frontier Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that appear to open a genuinely new direction, paradigm, or field.

items.title: RoPE Distinguishes Neither Positions Nor Tokens in Long Contexts, Provably
items.summary: RoPE is central to many frontier models, and a proof of intrinsic long-context failure is both broadly important now and conceptually new.
items.spotlight_comment: RoPE is central to many frontier models, and a proof of intrinsic long-context failure is both broadly important now and conceptually new.

items.title: Transformer-like Inference from Optimal Control
items.summary: Recovering transformer-like inference from optimal control is a notably original theoretical direction with paradigm-level flavor.
items.spotlight_comment: Recovering transformer-like inference from optimal control is a notably original theoretical direction with paradigm-level flavor.

items.title: Grokking as Structural Inference: Transformers Need Bayesian Lottery Tickets
items.summary: Its Bayesian structural-inference account of grokking offers a notably fresh mechanistic framing of transformer generalization dynamics.
items.spotlight_comment: Its Bayesian structural-inference account of grokking offers a notably fresh mechanistic framing of transformer generalization dynamics.

items.title: Structure Abstraction and Generalization in a Hippocampal-Entorhinal Inspired World Model
items.summary: The explicit hippocampal-entorhinal style separation of relational structure and episodic scenes for reusable world modeling suggests a potentially new research direction.
items.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
items.spotlight_comment: The explicit hippocampal-entorhinal style separation of relational structure and episodic scenes for reusable world modeling suggests a potentially new research direction.

paper_spotlight.label: Daily Hot Papers
paper_spotlight.description: Papers that feel broadly important to the day and belong in the hotspot paper feed.

items.title: DiLA: Disentangled Latent Action World Models
items.summary: Broadly important today: Disentangles content and structure to learn semantically structured latent actions while preserving world-model generation fidelity.
items.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Disentangles content and structure to learn semantically structured latent actions while preserving world-model generation fidelity.

items.title: STS: Efficient Sparse Attention with Speculative Token Sparsity
items.summary: Sparse attention for million-token agentic contexts is broadly important to current frontier inference efficiency, so it also fits the daily hotspot feed.
items.spotlight_comment: Sparse attention for million-token agentic contexts is broadly important to current frontier inference efficiency, so it also fits the daily hotspot feed.

items.title: Runtime-Orchestrated Second-Order Optimization for Scalable LLM Training
items.summary: Broadly important today: Makes second-order LLM training practical by runtime orchestration of optimizer state across GPU, CPU, and NVMe with asynchronous host-side updates.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Makes second-order LLM training practical by runtime orchestration of optimizer state across GPU, CPU, and NVMe with asynchronous host-side updates.

items.title: Rethinking Neural Network Learning Rates: A Stackelberg Perspective
items.summary: Broadly important today: Recasts layerwise learning-rate asymmetry as two-time-scale Stackelberg optimization with convergence and curvature analysis.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Recasts layerwise learning-rate asymmetry as two-time-scale Stackelberg optimization with convergence and curvature analysis.

items.title: IO-SVD: Input-Output Whitened SVD for Adaptive-Rank LLM Compression
items.summary: Broadly important today: Introduces KL-aware input-output whitening and heterogeneous rank allocation for post-training low-rank LLM compression.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Introduces KL-aware input-output whitening and heterogeneous rank allocation for post-training low-rank LLM compression.

items.title: H-Mem: A Novel Memory Mechanism for Evolving and Retrieving Agent Memory via a Hybrid Structure
items.summary: Broadly important today: A hybrid temporal-semantic tree plus knowledge graph models memory evolution, consolidation, and retrieval for agents over long time horizons.
items.primary_topic_label: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: A hybrid temporal-semantic tree plus knowledge graph models memory evolution, consolidation, and retrieval for agents over long time horizons.

x_buzz.title: Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Model Release on Arena
x_buzz.summary: ByteDance Seed announced the release of the Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview model on Arena. This is a named model release and appears to make an early preview version available for evaluation or testing.
x_buzz.linked_topic: Seed 2.0 Launch Puts ByteDance Seed Into Real-Time Multimodal Competition

x_buzz.title: GLM-5 announced as Zhipu AI's new flagship foundation model for agent engineering
x_buzz.summary: Zhipu AI announced GLM-5 as a flagship base model aimed at agent engineering, highlighting open-source SOTA performance in coding and agent capabilities. The page also claims strong results on SWE-bench Verified and Terminal Bench 2.0, comparable to Claude Opus 4.5.
x_buzz.linked_topic: GLM-5 Becomes Zhipu AI’s Flagship Model for Agent Engineering

x_buzz.title: Introducing Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
x_buzz.summary: ByteDance Seed introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, a speech model focused on attentive listening, robust interference suppression, and more natural interaction. The announcement positions it as a new model launch in the company’s speech AI lineup.
x_buzz.linked_topic: Seed 2.0 Launch Puts ByteDance Seed Into Real-Time Multimodal Competition

x_buzz.title: Seed 2.0 Official Launch
x_buzz.summary: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The announcement marks a major foundation model release in the company’s product portfolio.
x_buzz.linked_topic: Seed 2.0 Launch Puts ByteDance Seed Into Real-Time Multimodal Competition

x_buzz.title: Seed Prover 1.5
x_buzz.summary: ByteDance Seed announced Seed Prover 1.5, describing it as delivering advanced mathematical reasoning through a novel agentic architecture. The news highlights a specialized model release aimed at reasoning-heavy tasks.
x_buzz.linked_topic: Seed 2.0 Launch Puts ByteDance Seed Into Real-Time Multimodal Competition
